data.title: Vail Resorts beats expectations, raises guidance
data.description:
Vail Resorts (NYSE:MTN) reported a slightly wider loss in the fiscal first quarter even as sales were marginally higher as costs associated with its two-year resource efficiency transformation plan offset an 8% price increase to its season passes.

For the quarter ended October 31, the company lost $4.61 per share, a penny lower from last year, but 44 cents better than Wall Street expected. Sales were up 0.7% to $260.3M, $10.5M above expectations.

“Our first fiscal quarter historically operates at a loss, given that our North American and European mountain resorts are generally not open for ski season. The quarter's results were driven by winter operations in Australia and summer activities in North America,” CEO Kirsten Lynch said, adding that favorable conditions have allowed some resorts to be opened earlier than anticipated, including the back bowls at Vail Mountain, opening the earliest in the season since 2018.

By segment, mountain net revenue increased 0.5% to $800K driven by an increase in summer visitation to North American resorts, while mountain EBITDA loss of $144.1M widened 3.3% from last year.

Lodging segment revenue increased 6.9% on positive weather conditions in the Grand Teton region, and higher dining and golf revenue. Lodging EBITDA increased to $4.3M from a loss of $236K last year.

Resort net revenue, which combines the mountain and lodging segments, increased 2.3% to $260.2M, while EBITDA loss for the segment narrowed slightly to a loss of $139.7M.

Looking ahead to the fiscal year ending July 31, 2025, Vail Resorts (MTN) raised its net income guidance to $240M to $316M from prior guidance of $224M to $300M, largely due to the sale of real estate. The current guidance assumes a continuation of the current economic environment, normal weather conditions for the 2024/2025 North American and European ski season and the 2025 Australian ski season, and foreign exchange rates.

Shares are up 3% in after-hours trading.

data.title: The Bank of N.T. Butterfield & Son announces new $100M share repurchase program
data.description:
• The Bank of N.T. Butterfield & Son Limited (NYSE:NTB) board has approved a $100M ordinary share repurchase program.
  • The Bank is authorized to purchase up to 2.7M of its ordinary shares through December 31, 2025.
  • The new share repurchase program will come into effect on January 1, 2025 with the termination of the current 2.1M share repurchase program that was announced on July 22, 2024.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Oracle in charts: Revenue from Americas rises most Y/Y among geographies in FQ2
Crane Company announces promotion of Alejandro Alcala to COO
data.description:
• Crane Company (NYSE:CR) has promoted Alejandro Alcala to chief operating officer, effective immediately. 
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Dover announces CFO retirement; names successor
data.description:
• Dover Corporation (NYSE:DOV) announced that Brad Cerepak, senior vice president and chief financial officer plans to retire on January 31, 2025.
  • Christopher Woenker, CFO of the company's engineered products and climate & sustainability technologies segments, will succeed Cerepak as senior vice president and CFO. 
  • DOV shares were up over 5% in after hours trading on Monday. 
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Gladstone Investment plans to issue notes due 2030
data.description:
• Gladstone Investment (NASDAQ:GAIN) on Monday announced that it plans to make a public offering of notes due 2030.
  • The company expects to list the notes on the Nasdaq Global Select Market under the trading symbol GAINI within 30 days of issuance.
  • The company intends to repay a portion of the amount outstanding under its revolving credit facility, to fund new investment opportunities, and for other general corporate purposes.
  • GAIN -0.43% after hours to $13.75.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Phreesia GAAP EPS of -$0.25 beats by $0.03, revenue of $106.8M beats by $0.48M
data.description:
• Phreesia press release (NYSE:PHR): Q3 GAAP EPS of -$0.25 beats by $0.03.
  • Revenue of $106.8M (+16.6% Y/Y) beats by $0.48M.
  • FY25 Outlook: We are narrowing our revenue outlook for fiscal 2025 to a range of $418 million to $420 million from a previous range of $416 million to $426 million, implying year-over-year growth of 17% to 18%.
  • We are updating our Adjusted EBITDA outlook for fiscal 2025 to a range of $34 million to $36 million from a previous range of $26 million to $31 million. Our outlook reflects our strong performance in the fiscal third quarter and our continued focus on margin improvement.
  • We are maintaining our expectation for AHSCs to reach approximately 4,200 for fiscal 2025, compared to 3,601 in fiscal 2024. We are maintaining our expectation for total revenue per AHSC to increase in fiscal 2025 compared to the $98,944 we achieved in fiscal 2024.
  • FY26 Outlook: We are introducing our revenue outlook for fiscal 2026. We expect revenue to be in the range of $472 million to $482 million. The revenue range provided for fiscal 2026 assumes no additional revenue from potential future acquisitions completed between now and January 31, 2026.
  • We are introducing our Adjusted EBITDA outlook for fiscal 2026. We expect Adjusted EBITDA to be in the range of $78 million to $88 million. The Adjusted EBITDA range provided for fiscal 2026 assumes continued improvement in operating leverage across the Company through focusing on efficiency.
  • We expect AHSCs to reach approximately 4,500 in fiscal 2026. Additionally, we expect total revenue per AHSC in fiscal 2026 to increase from fiscal 2025.
  • We believe our $81.7 million in cash and cash equivalents as of October 31, 2024, along with cash generated in our normal operations, gives us sufficient flexibility to reach our fiscal 2025 and fiscal 2026 outlook. Additionally, our available borrowing capacity under our credit facility with Capital One provides us with an additional source of capital to pursue future growth opportunities not incorporated into our fiscal 2025 and fiscal 2026 outlook. As of October 31, 2024, we have no borrowings outstanding under our credit facility
  • Shares +3.74%.

data.title: Brookdale Senior Living occupancy increases to 79.5% in November
data.description:
• Brookdale Senior Living (NYSE:BKD) on Monday reported that its November weighted average occupancy increased 110 basis points year-over-year to 79.5% in 2024.
  • In November, its monthly weighted average occupancy increased 10 basis points sequentially from October, exceeded 80% for the fourth consecutive month.
  • Fourth quarter-to-date move-ins and move-outs improved to the prior year comparable period, supporting a 100 basis point weighted average occupancy increase over 2023 fourth quarter-to-date, it added.
  • BKD +0.91% after hours to $5.53.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: GSK's belantamab mafodotin reduced death risk by 42% in study
data.description:
GSK (NYSE:GSK) reported that a study showed its drug belantamab mafodotin was able to reduce the risk of death by 42% in patients with multiple myeloma at or after first relapse.

The study is evaluating belantamab mafodotin in combination with bortezomib plus dexamethasone, or BVd, versus daratumumab in combination with bortezomib plus dexamethasone, or DVd, as a second line or later treatment for relapsed or refractory multiple myeloma.

A planned interim analysis of the study, called DREAMM-7, showed a statistically significant 42% reduction in the risk of death among patients receiving the belantamab mafodotin combination versus the daratumumab-based comparator. The median follow up was 39.4 months.

GSK (GSK) added that although the median overall survival, or mOS, was not reached in either study arm, the projected mOS for BVd is 84 months compared to 51 months for DVd, according to a statement.

The U.K. drugmaker also said the belantamab mafodotin combination showed statistically significant superiority on the key secondary endpoint of minimal residual disease negativity.

The data was presented at the American Society of Hematology annual meeting in San Diego.

GSK (GSK) said filings for belantamab mafodotin combinations for the treatment of relapsed or refractory multiple myeloma have been accepted by regulators in the United States, European Union, Japan, China, United Kingdom, Canada and Switzerland.

data.title: Casey's General Stores boosts profit in FQ2 despite sales dip
data.description:
Casey's General Stores (NASDAQ:CASY) traded slightly lower as investors took in the retailer's FQ2 earnings report and guidance update.

Revenue fell 2.7% year-over-year to $3.95 billion to miss the consensus expectation by $70 million. Inside same-store sales increased 4.0% compared to a year ago, while same-store fuel gallons were down 0.6%

"Casey's delivered a strong second quarter highlighted by robust inside gross profit growth," stated CEO Darren Rebelez. He highlighted that inside same-store sales were driven by the prepared food and dispensed beverage category, with hot sandwiches and cold dispensed beverages performing exceptionally well. Rebelez said the fuel team achieved over $0.40 cents per gallon fuel margin, while outpacing the relevant geographic market in same-store fuel gallons.

EBITDA came in at $348.9 million vs. $305.9 million a year ago. EPS was reported at $4.85 vs. $4.27 consensus and $4.24 a year ago.

For fiscal year 2025, Casey's (CASY) expects EBITDA to increase at least 10%. Total operating expenses are expected to increase 11% to 13% for the fiscal year, including approximately $25 to $30 million in one-time deal and integration costs, while same-store operating expense excluding credit card fees are expected to only increase 2% for the year. The guidance includes the anticipated impact of the Fikes acquisition.

Shares of Casey’s General Stores (CASY) were down 1.10% in postmarket action on Monday, after trimming 0.99% during the regular session.
